free_text: Differential cross sections for the 12C(He3,p)14N
reaction.
2 MV and 6 MV Van de Graaff accelerator.
Data digitizing error
Data in Sub 003 for 150 degr. were
redigitized. Sub 002 was split into eleven subentries,
Sub 003 was split into three subentries.
Nuclear emulsion reaction chamber was used.
The targets were prepared by heating a 5 micro inch
foil in the presence of methyl iodide (CH3I) vapor,
depositing the carbon onto the foil. The target used
at 2.0 MeV was about 180 keV thick for the incident 3He
particles, and that used at 4.5 MeV was about 200 keV
thick for the incident 3He particles.
Angular digitizing error
Errors from the graphic bars include an
uncertainty in the measurement of the solid angle, an
estimated uncertainty in counting the tracks, and the
statistical deviations.
Uncertainty in the measurement of the solid
angle
Uncertainty in counting the tracks

Fig.8
Thin CsI(Tl) crystals mounted on multiplier
phototubes.
A thin (about 40 keV thick for 2 MeV 3He particles)
evaporated target was used. This target was weighed in
order to determine the absolute differential cross
section.
Energy digitizing error
The uncertainty arises largely from the
uncertainty in the measurement of the thickness
of the target.
